skill.name: workflow
skill.description: Manages handoffs between PM and implementation agents and helps apply approved review fixes.
skill.summary: Creates structured PM, implementation, and review-fix workflow handoffs.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks, weak cryptography, and high entropy, but review found the crypto and entropy alerts are false positives. The only meaningful risk is Bash availability and fixed local Git inspection guidance; no evidence found for exfiltration, network access, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Bash Access With Fixed Local Git Commands
low_findings.description: The skill declares Bash access and instructs use of fixed local Git inspection commands. The commands are not assembled from user input, so this is a low-risk operational capability rather than command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and Git commands are explicit in the files. The low severity is based on fixed command text and no evidence of user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged description lines as weak cryptography, but those lines contain skill descriptions only. No cryptographic API, hash algorithm, encryption logic, or credential handling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged lines found plain YAML descriptions. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged high entropy at documentation file starts. The files are readable Markdown with Japanese prose and YAML frontmatter, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The complete files are short readable Markdown documents. No encoded payloads, compressed blocks, or hidden instructions were present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Ruby shell backtick detections occur in Markdown code fences, inline file names, and inline command examples. Review found no Ruby source code or executable backtick interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown documentation and examples. The only real command guidance is the fixed Git inspection noted separately.

content.value_statement: Multi-agent development can lose context when tasks move between planning, implementation, and review. This skill creates structured handoffs and controlled review-fix workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users.
actual_capabilities: Routes workflow requests to auto-fix, handoff-to-impl, or handoff-to-pm guidance.
Generates PM-to-implementation task requests with background, scope, constraints, and acceptance criteria.
Generates implementation-to-PM completion reports with changes, tests, risks, and next actions.
Guides approved fixes for review findings after previewing changes for user approval.
Uses local project context such as Plans.md and Git status when available.
limitations: It does not replace the separate 2-agent setup skill.
It depends on accurate Plans.md content and clear task ownership.
It does not verify business requirements beyond the context provided.
Bash-enabled workflows should be reviewed before running commands in sensitive repositories.

use_cases.title: Prepare implementation work
use_cases.description: Create a clear task request before passing planned work to an implementation agent.

use_cases.title: Report completed changes
use_cases.description: Summarize implementation results, touched files, tests, and remaining risks for a PM review.

use_cases.title: Apply review fixes
use_cases.description: Turn review comments into a controlled fix plan with a preview before edits are applied.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff for this task so an implementation agent can start work.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a scoped implementation request
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the current plan and create an implementation request with goals, scope, acceptance criteria, and constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a PM completion report
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the completed implementation for PM review, including changed files, tests, acceptance criteria, risks, and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan approved review fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the latest comments, identify safe fixes, preview the planned edits, and wait for approval before applying changes.

output_examples.input: Create a handoff for the login form validation task.
output_examples.output: A PM-to-implementation request with context, expected result, acceptance criteria, target files, scope limits, and constraints.

output_examples.input: Implementation is finished. Report back to PM.
output_examples.output: A completion report covering the summary, user-visible changes, changed files, validation results, acceptance status, risks, and next steps.

output_examples.input: Fix the issues from the review comments.
output_examples.output: A reviewed fix plan that lists fixable issues, previews proposed changes, requests approval, and then reports completed corrections.

best_practices: Keep Plans.md current before creating handoffs.
Include acceptance criteria and explicit non-goals in every implementation request.
Review proposed fixes before allowing edits in production or security-sensitive repositories.
anti_patterns: Using the skill when a full two-agent environment still needs setup.
Sending vague requests without task scope, files, or acceptance criteria.
Applying review fixes without checking the preview and expected behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill help coordinate?
faq.answer: It coordinates handoffs between PM and implementation roles, plus controlled review-fix workflows.

faq.question: Does it create a two-agent setup?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that two-agent setup should use a separate 2agent skill.

faq.question: Can it edit files?
faq.answer: Yes. The auto-fix workflow can apply edits after previewing the planned changes and receiving user approval.

faq.question: Does it run external commands?
faq.answer: It allows Bash and mentions local Git status and diff inspection for completion reporting.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: No network behavior was found, but users should review Bash usage and proposed edits before approval.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
